warning: AI-assisted monitoring. Verify against official legislation before product approval, export, label declaration, or customer compliance confirmation.

rules.foodCategory: All foods, including seasonings and snack products
rules.limit: Verification required via GSFA Online Database
rules.sourceTitle: Food Additives DB (GSFA Online) | CODEXALIMENTARIUS FAO-WHO
rules.notes: The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions for food additive use. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpreting data.
rules.evidenceQuote: The "Codex General Standard for Food Additives" (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets forth the conditions under which permitted food additives may be used in all foods

rules.foodCategory: Food and feed commodities, including seasonings and snack products
rules.limit: Maximum levels (MLs) established by Codex must be adhered to. Verification required via relevant Codex texts.
rules.notes: Contaminants are substances not intentionally added to food. Codex MLs represent the maximum concentration legally permitted to protect human health. The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes these levels.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Codex maximum level (ML) for a contaminant in a food or feed commodity is the maximum concentration of that substance recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission to be legally permitted in that commodity.

rules.foodCategory: Food and feed, including seasonings and snack products
rules.notes: This standard provides general provisions for contaminants and toxins in food and feed.
rules.evidenceQuote: CXS 193-1995 General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed

rules.parameter: Approved food additives and conditions of use
rules.notes: Food additives must be approved for use in the EU and comply with their conditions of use as listed in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.
rules.evidenceQuote: This database can serve as a tool to inform about the food additives approved for use in food in the EU and their conditions of use. It is based on the Union list of food additives. This list is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

rules.parameter: Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
rules.sourceTitle: Microbiological criteria - Food Safety - European Commission
rules.notes: Food safety criteria and process hygiene criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins, and metabolites must be met as defined in Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005.
rules.evidenceQuote: Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 on microbiological criteria for foods, applicable from 1 January 2006, lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites, such as Salmonella , Listeria monocytogenes , Enterobacter sakazakii , staphylococcal enterotoxins and histamine in specific foods.

rules.parameter: Permitted food additives and their limits
rules.limit: Verification required as per GSO 9:2022 and related standards
rules.notes: GSO 9:2022, as a labelling standard, would require the listing of ingredients, including food additives. Specific limits for additives would be detailed within GSO 9:2022 or referenced standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.notes: As a general labelling standard, GSO 9:2022 is expected to include requirements for the declaration of allergens on prepackaged food labels.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.parameter: General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
rules.notes: This regulation sets out the general requirements for labels of prepackaged foodstuffs, which would include mandatory information, presentation, and language.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-1:2013 - Shelf life of food products - Part 1: Mandatory shelf life
rules.notes: This regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for various food products, which must be declared on the label.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-1:2013 لائحة فنية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الأول : فترات الصلاحية الإلزامية

rules.sourceTitle: GSO 150-2:2013 - Shelf life of food products - Part 2: Optional shelf life
rules.notes: This standard provides guidelines for optional shelf life periods for food products.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 150-2:2013 مواصفة قياسية خليجية --> فترات صلاحية المنتجات الغذائية - الجزء الثاني : فترات الصلاحية الاختيارية

rules.limit: Verification required as per GSO 9:2022 and related standards
rules.notes: GSO 9:2022, as a labelling standard, is expected to include requirements for nutritional information, which would cover sodium content. Specific limits or declaration thresholds would need verification.
rules.evidenceQuote: GSO 9:2022 لائحة فنية خليجية --> بطاقات المواد الغذائية المعبأة

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Regulations and specific limits for food additives must be verified through official MHLW publications.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food Additives

rules.parameter: Food and additive standards and criteria
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: The MHLW establishes criteria for methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food/additives, and standards for their ingredients. Food/additives must conform to these established criteria and standards. Businesses must verify these standards.
rules.evidenceQuote: The Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare may establish the criteria for the methods of producing, processing, using, cooking, or preserving food or additives... or may establish standards for the ingredients of food or additives... food or additives must not be produced... using methods that do not conform to such criteria; food or additives that do not conform to such standards must not be produced... or sold.

rules.parameter: Specifications and Standards for Food and Food Additives
rules.sourceTitle: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act
rules.notes: Detailed specifications and standards for food and food additives, including various limits, are established under the Food Sanitation Act and must be verified.
rules.evidenceQuote: Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act

rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and generally consumed substances used as additives) and preparations/food containing them are prohibited from sale, production, import, processing, use, storage, or display for sale, unless the MHLW specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: Additives (excluding natural flavoring agents and things that have generally been served for human consumption, and are used as additives) and preparations and food containing additives must not be sold... except for cases that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.sourceTitle: Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare: Food Safety Information
rules.notes: Japan operates a Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food. Specific limits must be verified through official MHLW publications.
rules.evidenceQuote: Positive List System for Agricultural Chemical Residues in Food

rules.parameter: Contamination or foreign substances causing harm
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food or additives likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are likely to cause harm to human health due to contamination or the addition of foreign substances or on other grounds

rules.parameter: Residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, and pharmaceuticals for animals
rules.limit: Prohibition if exceeding MHLW specified quantity
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food containing residues of agricultural chemicals, feed additives, or pharmaceuticals for animals in quantities exceeding those specified by the MHLW as unlikely to cause harm is prohibited from production, import, processing, use, cooking, preservation for sale, or sale. This does not apply if specific standards for residual limits are established under Article 13, paragraph (1).
rules.evidenceQuote: Food in which substances that are the ingredients of agricultural chemicals... feed additives... and pharmaceutical products... remain in a quantity exceeding the quantity that the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies as unlikely to cause harm to human health... must not be produced... or sold

rules.parameter: Toxic or harmful substances in food/additives
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food or additives containing or suspected to contain toxic or harmful substances are prohibited, unless the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies them as unlikely to cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which contain or bear toxic or harmful substances or are suspected to contain or bear such substances; provided, however, that this does not apply when the Minister of Health, Labour and Welfare specifies that the substances are unlikely to cause harm to human health

rules.parameter: Food safety responsibility and record keeping
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food business operators must endeavor to ensure the safety of food, obtain necessary knowledge/skills, ensure raw material safety, conduct voluntary inspections, and keep records of suppliers. They must also take prompt measures for hazards, including providing records to authorities and disposing of hazardous food.
rules.evidenceQuote: A food business operator... must endeavor to ensure the safety of the food... make a record of any necessary information... and retain the record

rules.parameter: Notification of harm from designated ingredients
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Businesses handling food containing MHLW-designated ingredients requiring special attention must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc., without delay if they obtain information that the food causes or may cause harm to human health.
rules.evidenceQuote: A business that handles food containing any ingredient or material which requires special attention... must notify the relevant prefectural governor, etc. of that information without delay

rules.parameter: Clean and sanitary handling of food and additives
rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food and additives meant for sale must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed, and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food or additives which are meant to be sold... must be collected, produced, processed, used, cooked, stored, transported, displayed and delivered in a clean and sanitary manner.

rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food or additives contaminated with pathogens or suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health, are prohibited.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are contaminated with pathogens or are suspected to be so, and which are likely to cause harm to human health

rules.effectiveDate: Act No. 233 of 1947 (Last Version: Act No. 46 of 2018)
rules.notes: Food or additives that are rotten, deteriorated, or immature are prohibited from sale, collection, production, import, processing, use, cooking, storage, or display for sale, unless specified as unlikely to cause harm and recognized as edible.
rules.evidenceQuote: those which are rotten or have deteriorated, or are immature; provided, however, that this does not apply to those which are unlikely to cause harm to human health in general and are recognized as edible

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.parameter: Combined Class II chemical preservatives (e.g., sorbic acid, benzoic acid)
rules.notes: Use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to determine permitted levels when two or more are used.
rules.evidenceQuote: If two or more Class II chemical preservatives are to be used in the food... use the Calculation Tool for Class II Chemical Preservatives to calculate the permitted levels.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA and are permitted if there is technological justification and they do not present health risks to consumers.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives must be assessed for safety by SFA before they can be used in food sold in Singapore.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of a non-permitted additive.
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food product contains an additive that is not permitted, do not import or sell it.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Food businesses must use the Food Additives Search tool or download the List of Permitted Food Additives to check if additives are allowed.
rules.evidenceQuote: Download the List of Permitted Food Additives or use the Food Additives Search tool to check if your additives are allowed.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.notes: Different limits apply depending on the additive and food product type. Refer to the Guidance Information on Requirements for Use of Food Additives.
rules.evidenceQuote: Different limits apply, depending on the additive and the type of food products.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.limit: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.notes: Contaminants can occur during production, manufacturing, treatment, packing, packaging, transport, or holding, or as a result of environmental contamination.
rules.evidenceQuote: Contaminants (also known as incidental constituents) in food sold in Singapore must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks
rules.sourceTitle: Regulatory Limits for Contaminants in Food
rules.evidenceQuote: If your food or food products contain contaminants or incidental constituents that exceed the regulatory limits, do not import or sell them.

rules.foodCategory: seasoning powder, seasoning sauce, snack seasoning, ready-to-eat snacks

rules.sourceTitle: Food Additive Listings; Substances Added to Food (formerly EAFUS)
rules.notes: The Substances Added to Food inventory is a partial list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Food additives and color additives that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 172, 173 and Parts 73, 74 respectively), and flavoring substances evaluated by FEMA* and JECFA*. Generally Recognized as Safe ("GRAS") substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Parts 182 and 184). Substances approved for specific uses in foods prior to September 6, 1958, known as prior-sanctioned substances (21 CFR Part 181).

rules.parameter: Prohibited substances and delisted color additives
rules.notes: Listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as 'PROHIBITED' or 'PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS' and delisted color additives (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30).
rules.evidenceQuote: Prohibited substances that are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189) as prohibited from use in food (labeled as "PROHIBITED" or "PROHIBITED WITH EXCEPTIONS"). Delisted color additives in FDA regulations (21 CFR 81.10 and/or 81.30) (labeled as "DELISTED").

rules.notes: A threshold is a value below which it is unlikely that a food allergic individual would experience an adverse effect.
rules.evidenceQuote: At this time, the FDA has not established a threshold level for any allergens.

rules.limit: Milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame.
rules.notes: Sesame was added as the 9th major food allergen by the FASTER Act, effective Jan 1, 2023.
rules.evidenceQuote: The major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. On April 23, 2021, the Food Allergy Safety, Treatment, Education, and Research (FASTER) Act was signed into law, declaring sesame as the 9th major food allergen recognized by the United States. The change was effective on January 1, 2023.

rules.notes: This is a Guidance Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: 3-Monochloropropane-1,2-Diol (3-MCPD) Acid-hydrolyzed protein and Asian-style sauces Guidance Level 1 ppm CPG 500.500

rules.foodCategory: Brazil nuts, Foods, Peanuts and peanut products, Pistachio nuts
rules.notes: This is an Action Level.
rules.evidenceQuote: Aflatoxins, total Brazil nuts Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.200; Aflatoxins, total Foods Action Level 20 ppb CPG 555.400; Aflatoxins, total Peanuts and peanut products Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.375; Aflatoxins, total Pistachio nuts Action Level 20 ppb CPG 570.500

rules.limit: Must comply with published contaminant levels (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels).
rules.notes: Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 CFR parts 109 and 509. Users are responsible for being informed about changes.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA has published a variety of contaminant levels for poisonous or deleterious substances in human food in various guidance documents and regulations for industry. For ease of reference, the Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool provides a consolidated list of these levels. Contaminant levels listed in the transparency tool include tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, derived intervention levels, recommended maximum levels, and advisory levels. Tolerances and action levels are defined in 21 Code of Federal Regulations (CFR) parts 109 and 509.

rules.limit: Must meet a defined standard for gluten content. Compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods bearing the claim.
rules.evidenceQuote: On August 2, 2013, the FDA issued a final rule defining “gluten-free” for food labeling... On August 12, 2020, the FDA issued a final rule to establish compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods, or foods that contain fermented or hydrolyzed ingredients, bearing the “gluten-free” claim.

rules.limit: Not required by law; can only be used to address unavoidable cross-contact if good manufacturing practices are incorporated and precautions taken. Must be truthful and not misleading.
rules.notes: Should not be a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices.
rules.evidenceQuote: Consumers may also see advisory statements such as “may contain [allergen] or “produced in a facility that also uses [allergen].” Such statements are not required by law and can be used to address unavoidable “cross-contact,” only if manufacturers have incorporated good manufacturing processes in their facility and have taken every precaution to avoid cross-contact... FDA guidance and regulations for the food industry states that advisory statements should not be used as a substitute for adhering to current good manufacturing practices and must be truthful and not misleading.

rules.limit: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label either in parentheses following the ingredient name OR immediately after or next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement.
rules.notes: Examples: 'lecithin (soy)' or 'Contains wheat, milk, and soy.'
rules.evidenceQuote: The allergen's food source must be declared at least once on the food label in one of two ways. The name of the food source of a major food allergen must appear: In parentheses following the name of the ingredient. Examples: “lecithin (soy),” “flour (wheat),” and “whey (milk)” — OR — Immediately after or next to the list of ingredients in a “contains” statement. Example: “Contains wheat, milk, and soy.”

rules.limit: Establishments must implement allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens, as per 21 CFR part 117. Written procedures are required.
rules.notes: Products lacking required allergen information or inadvertently containing an allergen due to cross-contact are considered misbranded or adulterated.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA’s “Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food” rule (CGMP & PC rule, 21 CFR part 117) establishes requirements applicable to establishments that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food. The CGMP & PC rule includes requirements for allergen preventive controls to prevent allergen cross-contact in manufacturing and packaging and to prevent undeclared allergens. For example, the FDA requires facilities to put written procedures in place to control allergen cross-contact between products that contain allergens and those that are not supposed to contain them and to ensure that the products are accurately labeled with respect to allergens.

rules.limit: Must be declared on the label.
rules.notes: Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.
rules.evidenceQuote: Color additives made from cochineal extract and carmine, which are derived from insects, have been identified as allergenic substances that must be declared on the label of all food and cosmetic products.

rules.notes: Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: The FDA requires all products containing FD&C Yellow No. 5 to identify it on their labels so consumers who are sensitive to the dye can avoid it.

rules.limit: Must be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.
rules.notes: The common or usual name of an ingredient may identify the allergen's food source.
rules.evidenceQuote: FALCPA requires that foods or ingredients that contain a "major food allergen" be specifically labeled with the name of the allergen source.

rules.limit: The type of tree nut, and the species of fish and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.
rules.evidenceQuote: The law also requires that the type of tree nut (for example, almonds, pecans, walnuts), and the species of fish (for example, bass, flounder, cod) and Crustacean shellfish (for example, crab, lobster, shrimp) to be declared.

rules.limit: Must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration is ≥10 total sulfites.
rules.notes: Required due to adverse reactions (e.g., asthma) in sensitive individuals.
rules.evidenceQuote: Various sulfiting agents, including sodium bisulfite, are allowed as food ingredients. But due in part to adverse reactions to them, such as asthma in sensitive individuals, they must be declared on food labels when present in food and the concentration in the food is ≥10 parts per million total sulfites.

rules.foodCategory: Imported processed foods, seasoning, and snack products
rules.parameter: Imported goods (with HS codes) subject to state inspection for food safety
rules.limit: Verification required as per the official list
rules.sourceTitle: 28/2026/TT-BCT Ban hành Danh mục các mặt hàng nhập khẩu (kèm theo mã số HS) thực hiện kiểm tra nhà nước về an toàn thực phẩm thuộc trách nhiệm quản lý nhà nước của Bộ Công Thương
rules.notes: This circular establishes a list of imported goods (with HS codes) that are subject to state inspection for food safety. Compliance with all relevant food safety regulations (including additive, microbiology, and contaminant limits) must be verified for items on this list.
rules.evidenceQuote: Danh mục các mặt hàng nhập khẩu (kèm theo mã số HS) thực hiện kiểm tra nhà nước về an toàn thực phẩm
